title: LoopbackRadio::receive ignores timeout_ms parameter
description:
File: rust/lichen-hal/src/loopback.rs:91-107

The receive() function ignores the timeout_ms parameter entirely (marked with underscore as _timeout_ms). When no packet is available, it returns None immediately instead of waiting for the specified timeout.

This violates the Radio trait contract and creates inconsistent behavior - MockRadio in lichen-embassy/mock.rs correctly sleeps for timeout_ms, but LoopbackRadio does not.

Impact: Tests using LoopbackRadio behave differently than production code. A test passing with LoopbackRadio might fail with real hardware if it depends on timing/timeout behavior.

Fix: Add async sleep/wait for timeout_ms before returning None, or at minimum poll with timeout.
